info.description: A comprehensive RESTful API backend for the Sizzle & Rhythm food delivery platform built with PHP, supporting vendor management, customer ordering, payment processing, and real-time operations.
info.title: Sizzle & Rhythm API

bearerAuth.description: Enter your JWT token in the format: Bearer {token}

post.description: Registers a new user with email and password. Phone is optional. Sends OTP for email verification.

message.example: Your registration was successful! Kindly verify your email to proceed.

post.description: Verify user's email address using OTP code sent via email

message.example: Your account has been verified successfully!

post.description: Resend verification OTP to user's email or phone
Authenticate user with email, phone, or username and password. Returns JWT token.

useridLogin.summary: Login with userid (email, phone, or username)

post.description: Logout user (client should remove JWT token)
Get a new JWT token using current valid token

get.description: Returns Google authorization URL to redirect user for OAuth login. Copy the authUrl from response and paste in browser.

message.example: Redirect user to this URL to authorize with Google

get.description: Handles Google OAuth callback with authorization code. After authorizing with Google, you'll be redirected here automatically.

post.description: Authenticate user using Google ID token (for mobile apps and SPAs using Google Sign-In SDK)

idToken.description: Google ID token from Google Sign-In SDK

post.description: Send a password reset OTP to user's email or phone.
Verify the OTP sent to user's email or phone for password reset.

message.example: OTP verified. You may now reset your password.

post.description: Update user's password after successful OTP verification.

message.example: Password updated successfully. You may now log in.

get.description: Retrieve the authenticated user's profile information.

post.description: Update the authenticated user's profile information (name, phone, etc).
Change the authenticated user's password.

get.description: Retrieve list of available and configured payment gateways

post.description: Initialize a payment transaction with the specified gateway

get.description: Verify a payment transaction using the transaction reference

post.description: Verify a payment transaction using the transaction reference (alternative POST method)

get.description: Retrieve payment transaction history for authenticated user

post.description: Handle webhook notifications from payment gateways

get.description: Handle user redirect after payment completion

parameters.name: redirect
parameters.description: Set to 'web' to redirect to frontend URL

post.description: Configure payment gateway credentials and settings (Admin only)

user_id.description: User ID for user-specific config (null for system-wide)

post.description: Test payment gateway connection with provided credentials (Admin only)

get.description: Retrieve payment gateway configuration (Admin only)
Retrieve payment transaction analytics and statistics (Admin only)

post.description: Admin creates a new user, vendor, or admin account. If password is provided, the admin sets it and the user receives credentials via email. If password is omitted, the user receives an onboarding email with a link to set their own password.

username.description: Unique username (3-30 chars, letters, numbers and underscores only)

password.description: If omitted, user will set their own password via email link

get.description: Paginated list of all users with optional filters by role, status and search term.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by name, email or phone

get.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific user by ID.

put.description: Admin updates a user's profile fields (first_name, last_name, phone, role, is_active).

post.description: Admin resets a user's password. If password is provided, that value is used. Otherwise a secure random password is generated. The user is notified via email in both cases.

password.description: Optional. If omitted a random password is generated.

post.description: Toggles a user's is_active flag. Admins cannot deactivate their own account. If the account is re-activated, the user receives a notification email.
Sends a test email to verify that the SMTP configuration is working. The email is sent immediately (not queued).

message.example: Test email sent successfully to test@example.com

post.description: Public endpoint used by new users who received an onboarding email to set their own password. Requires the one-time token and user UUID from the email link.

token.description: One-time setup token from the email link

uid.description: User UUID from the email link

password.description: The password the user wants to set

200.description: Password set successfully. User can now log in.

400.description: Invalid/used setup link or weak password

post.description: Set up the food vendor store profile. Called once when a vendor first registers. Supports JSON body or multipart/form-data (when uploading logo/banner). For multipart, send cuisine_type, tags, and opening_hours as JSON strings. Requires vendor role.

description.example: Authentic Nigerian home-cooked meals made fresh daily
Authentic Nigerian home-cooked meals made fresh daily

tags.description: JSON string array, e.g. ["spicy","grills"]

400.description: Validation error or profile already exists

get.description: Retrieve the authenticated vendor's full store profile including user info.

put.description: Update any store profile fields. All fields optional — only provided fields are updated. Changing business_name auto-regenerates the slug.

post.description: Upload or replace the store's logo image. Max 2MB. Accepts PNG, JPEG, WebP.

400.description: No file provided or invalid file type/size

post.description: Upload or replace the store's banner/cover image. Max 5MB. Accepts PNG, JPEG, WebP.

400.description: No file provided or invalid file type/size

put.description: Set the store's weekly opening hours. Each day requires an 'open' and 'close' time.

400.description: Invalid format — each day needs open/close times

post.description: Quick toggle the store between open and closed. No request body needed.

200.description: Store status toggled. Returns new is_open boolean.

put.description: Update delivery-related settings: fees, radius, availability, minimum order, etc.

get.description: Lists all active payment gateways with their configuration_fields, supported currencies/countries, and the vendor's current configuration status for each. Use this to guide vendors on what credentials to provide before calling the configure endpoint.

200.description: Available gateways with configuration fields and vendor setup status

configuration_fields.description: Each key is a credential field name. The value describes its type (text/password), label, and whether it's required.

is_configured.description: Whether the vendor has configured this gateway

post.description: Save payment gateway credentials and settings for the authenticated vendor. The vendor's user_id is automatically applied from the JWT token.

credentials.description: Gateway-specific credentials. Keys vary per gateway.

get.description: Retrieve the vendor's stored gateway configuration. Credential values are hidden — only the credential key names are returned.

post.description: Test a gateway connection with the provided credentials without saving. Useful for validating credentials before committing.

200.description: Gateway connection test successful — returns supported methods and currencies

400.description: Connection test failed or missing fields

get.description: Public endpoint to browse and search active food vendors. Filter by cuisine, city, open status, delivery availability. Paginated.

parameters.name: cuisine
parameters.description: Filter by cuisine type (e.g. Nigerian, Chinese)

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search store name, description, or vendor name

get.description: Public endpoint to view a food vendor's full store profile by their unique slug.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: The vendor's unique URL slug (e.g. mamas-kitchen)

post.description: Create a new category. Supports JSON body or multipart/form-data (when uploading an image). Categories are shared between inventory and menu via the 'type' field.

get.description: List all active categories for the vendor. Optionally filter by type.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter by type. 'inventory' returns inventory+both, 'menu' returns menu+both.

put.description: Update a category's name, description, type, sort_order, or is_active.

delete.description: Permanently delete a category. Fails if inventory or menu items still reference it.

409.description: Category in use — reassign items first

post.description: Upload or replace a category's image. Max 2MB. PNG/JPEG/WebP.
Add a new stock-keeping item. Supports JSON body or multipart/form-data (when uploading an image).

get.description: Paginated list of inventory items with extensive filters and contextual analytics summary. Analytics always reflect the same filters applied to the list.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by name, SKU, description, or supplier

parameters.name: low_stock
parameters.description: Show only low-stock items (qty > 0 but ≤ low_stock_level)

parameters.name: has_image
parameters.description: Filter items with or without images

parameters.name: unit
parameters.description: Filter by unit (e.g., pcs, kg, cartons)

parameters.name: is_active
parameters.description: Filter by active status. Default shows active only. Use 'all' for everything.

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Filter items created from this date (YYYY-MM-DD)

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Filter items created up to this date (YYYY-MM-DD)

200.description: Items list with analytics, category breakdown, and pagination

put.description: Update item details (not quantity — use /adjust for stock changes).

delete.description: Permanently remove an inventory item and its stock logs.

post.description: Add or remove stock. Creates an audit log entry. Types: restock (adds), sale/waste (subtracts), return (adds), correction (±).

quantity.description: Amount to adjust (always positive for restock/sale/waste/return)

200.description: Stock adjusted — returns updated item + adjustment details

get.description: Paginated audit trail of stock changes for a specific inventory item.

post.description: Upload or replace an inventory item's image. Max 2MB. PNG/JPEG/WebP.

get.description: Overview: total items, total stock value, low-stock count, expiring-soon count, and per-category breakdown.

post.description: Add a new item to the vendor's point-of-sale menu. Supports JSON body or multipart/form-data (when uploading an image). For multipart, send tags and options as JSON strings.

description.example: Smoky party-style jollof rice served with grilled chicken
Smoky party-style jollof rice served with grilled chicken

tags.description: JSON string array, e.g. ["spicy","popular"]

options.description: JSON string array of objects, e.g. [{"name":"Extra Chicken","price":500}]

get.description: Vendor views their own menu items. Supports filters and sorting.

put.description: Update a menu item's details, pricing, tags, options, etc.

post.description: Quick toggle a menu item between available and unavailable.
Upload or replace a menu item's image. Max 3MB. PNG/JPEG/WebP.

get.description: Public endpoint for customers to browse a vendor's available menu items by vendor slug or ID. Returns categories and paginated items.

200.description: Vendor info, menu categories, and paginated menu items

post.description: Admin creates a new POS payment method (e.g. Cash, Card, Transfer, Online).

get.description: Admin lists all POS payment methods (active and inactive).

put.description: Admin updates an existing POS payment method.

delete.description: Admin deletes a POS payment method. Fails if any orders reference it.

get.description: Returns all active payment methods with the vendor's enabled/disabled state and gateway configuration status.

200.description: Array of payment methods with vendor_enabled flag

post.description: Enable or disable a payment method for this vendor's POS. Online method requires a configured payment gateway.

200.description: Payment method toggled with new state

post.description: Create a new order with items. Items can reference menu_item_id or be ad-hoc (item_name + item_price). Optionally include payment at creation — payment_method_id must be enabled in the vendor's payment config. If fully paid and status is draft, the order auto-promotes to pending. Payment can also be processed later via the /payment endpoint.

payment_method_id.description: ID of a payment method enabled for this vendor. Optional — omit to leave order unpaid.

amount_paid.description: Amount the customer is paying. Defaults to total_amount if payment_method_id is provided but amount_paid is omitted.

gateway_reference.description: External gateway reference for online payments.

item_price.description: Required if no menu_item_id; optional override if menu_item_id given

201.description: Order created with items and optional payment

400.description: Validation error, menu item not found, or payment method not enabled

get.description: List vendor's POS orders with filtering by status, payment_status, archived state, date range, and search by order number / customer name / phone. Includes an analytics summary that reflects the same filters: total orders, status breakdown, payment breakdown, revenue, collected revenue, unpaid amount, total discounts, and average order value.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search order number, customer name or phone

200.description: Analytics summary + paginated orders with item_count and total_quantity

get.description: Get full order details including all line items, payment method, and timestamps.

post.description: Add new line items to an existing order (draft, pending, or preparing). Recalculates totals automatically.

404.description: Order not found or not editable

delete.description: Remove a single line item from an editable order. Recalculates totals and adjusts discount if necessary.

200.description: Updated order without the removed item

put.description: Transition order through the workflow: draft → pending → preparing → ready → completed. Cancelled orders cannot be modified. Completed orders get a completed_at timestamp.

post.description: Record payment for an order. For cash: pass amount_paid to calculate change. For card/transfer: amount_paid defaults to total. Auto-moves draft orders to pending when fully paid.

payment_method_id.description: Must be enabled for this vendor

amount_paid.description: Defaults to total_amount. For cash, pass actual amount received.

200.description: Payment recorded with payment_status (paid/partial) and change_due

400.description: Already paid, cancelled, or payment method not enabled

post.description: Apply a flat amount or percentage discount to an order in draft or pending status. Capped at subtotal.

200.description: Order with discount applied and recalculated total

404.description: Order not found or not editable

post.description: Archive or unarchive an order. Archived orders are hidden from the default list view but still accessible with ?archived=true or ?archived=all.

get.description: Get a comprehensive daily summary: total/completed/cancelled/active orders, revenue, discounts, collected vs unpaid, payment method breakdown, top selling items, and order type breakdown.

post.description: Create a new team member for your vendor store. Creates a user account (role=team_member) and links them to your vendor. If the email already belongs to an existing user, they are linked directly.

permissions.description: Custom permissions array. If omitted, defaults for the team_role are used.

password.description: Optional custom password. If omitted, a temporary password is generated.

201.description: Team member added successfully with user account created

400.description: Validation error (missing fields, invalid role, user already on a team)

409.description: Email already belongs to a non-team-member user

get.description: Get all team members for your vendor store. Optionally filter by status.

200.description: List of team members with user details

put.description: Update a team member's role and/or permissions.

delete.description: Permanently remove a team member. Sets their status to 'removed' and deactivates their user account.

get.description: Returns all available team roles, permission slugs, and the default permission sets for each role. Useful for building role assignment UIs.

200.description: Available roles, permissions, and role_defaults map

get.description: Retrieve full details of a single team member by ID.

200.description: Team member details with user info

post.description: Change a team member's status (active, suspended, invited, removed). Activating sets joined_at and enables the user account; suspending/removing disables it.
Add a customer to your vendor directory. Creates a new user account (role=customer) if no matching email/phone found, or links an existing user. Adds vendor-specific metadata (tags, notes).

get.description: Get paginated list of your vendor's customers. Supports search (name/email/phone), tag filter, and sorting.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by name, email, or phone

delete.description: Soft-delete a customer from your vendor directory. Sets is_active=0 on vendor_customers. The user account is NOT deleted.

get.description: Get aggregated customer stats: totals, active/inactive counts, revenue, average spend, highest spender, average orders, and top 5 customers by spend.
Retrieve full customer details including user profile and their 10 most recent POS orders.
Get full paginated order history for a specific customer, matched by their phone number against POS orders.

Single endpoint that returns all dashboard data: status cards (with % change vs previous period), sales graph, order volume graph, recent orders (all statuses, latest 10), top selling items, payment method breakdown, and order type breakdown. Graph mode adapts to the period: today/yesterday → daily (last 7 days), 7days → daily with day-name labels, this_month → weekly buckets, this_year → monthly buckets, date_range → auto (daily/weekly/monthly based on duration).

200.description: Full dashboard data with cards, graphs, pending orders, top items, payment and order type breakdowns

get.description: Returns status cards: total_orders, pending_orders, completed_orders, cancelled_orders, revenue (each with % change vs previous equivalent period), and low_stock_alert count.

Returns revenue graph for the selected period. Graph mode: today/yesterday → daily (last 7 days, date labels), 7days → daily (day-name labels like Mon, Tue), this_month → weekly buckets, this_year → monthly buckets, date_range → auto. Includes total revenue, previous period revenue, and percentage change.

200.description: Daily sales graph data with comparison

get.description: Returns order count graph (excluding cancelled) for the selected period. Graph mode: today/yesterday → daily (last 7 days, date labels), 7days → daily (day-name labels), this_month → weekly buckets, this_year → monthly buckets, date_range → auto.

200.description: Daily order volume graph data with comparison

get.description: Paginated list of recent orders across all statuses (draft, pending, preparing, ready, completed, cancelled), not archived. Optionally filter by a single status using ?status= query param. Each order includes its items list, customer info, totals, payment status, and timestamps.
Paginated list of inventory items where current quantity is at or below the low_stock_level threshold. Sorted by severity (lowest stock ratio first).
Returns the top selling menu items for the selected period, ranked by total quantity sold. Includes total sales amount and number of distinct orders.
Returns revenue breakdown by payment method (Cash, Card, Transfer, Online) for the selected period. Each method includes order count, total amount, and percentage of total revenue.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: Traditional email/password authentication endpoints

tags.name: OAuth
tags.description: Google OAuth authentication endpoints

tags.name: User
tags.description: User profile management endpoints

tags.name: Payment
tags.description: Payment processing and transaction management endpoints

tags.name: Payment Admin
tags.description: Payment gateway administration and analytics endpoints

tags.name: Admin - Accounts
tags.description: Admin account management — create users, update roles, reset passwords, toggle status

tags.name: Vendor Store
tags.description: Food vendor store management — set up store, update details, opening hours, delivery settings (requires vendor role)

tags.name: Vendor Payment Config
tags.description: Vendor payment gateway configuration — set up, retrieve, and test payment gateway credentials (requires vendor role)

tags.name: Vendor Categories
tags.description: Unified category management for both inventory and menu — create, list, update, delete categories (requires vendor role)

tags.name: Vendor Inventory
tags.description: Stock keeping — manage ingredients, supplies, raw materials. Track quantities, costs, low-stock alerts, and adjustment history (requires vendor role)

tags.name: Vendor Menu
tags.description: Point-of-sale menu management — create menu items, set prices, manage availability, upload images (requires vendor role)

tags.name: Vendors - Public
tags.description: Public food vendor discovery — browse stores, search menus, view vendor profiles

tags.name: POS Admin
tags.description: Admin management of POS payment methods — create, update, delete payment method types available to vendors

tags.name: POS Vendor
tags.description: Vendor POS payment method management — view available methods, enable/disable for your store

tags.name: POS Orders
tags.description: Point-of-sale order workflow — create orders, manage items, apply discounts, process payments, track status, archive orders, daily sales summary

tags.name: Vendor Team
tags.description: Vendor team management — add team members, assign roles and granular permissions, activate/suspend/remove members (requires vendor role)

tags.name: Vendor Customers
tags.description: Vendor customer directory — add customers, track spend/orders, tag and search customers, view order history, customer statistics (requires vendor role)

tags.name: Vendor Dashboard
tags.description: Vendor dashboard analytics — status cards, sales graph, order volume graph, pending orders, low stock alerts, top selling items, payment breakdown. All endpoints support date filtering (today, yesterday, 7days, this_month, date_range)
